changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "A Drink at Your Deathbed"

TextENGNew: ``...Anyway, that was a beautiful performance.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3004. The target was a relatively well-built boy, so I wasn't sure how you'd be able to take him out without leaving behind any evidence... I wouldn't have expected you to throw him out the window.``
``Excellent work, as we expected of you.``

``Hehe... Please, don't flatter me.@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3002. Thanks to all the time I've spent taking care of hospital patients, I'm able to understand just a little of their behavioral psychology.``
She knew it was flattery, but it felt good regardless.
After all, even she didn't expect her trick to work out so well without any help.
``I had to take responsibility for the window being left unlocked, but even that only amounted to a stern reprimanding.``

``The police shouldn't press charges for a death caused by workplace negligence.@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3005. And on top of that, even if management makes me take responsibility and fires me, nobody suspects a thing.``
``Indeed.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3007. As promised, you'll receive a suitable reward for executing this crime so perfectly.``
``Thank you very much.@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3007. I look forward to opening the bankbook after I arrive.@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3008. Hehe...``
...For that amount of money, she had no regrets about throwing away a job she worked at for so many years.
Being obligated to deal with hospital patients day and night, without rest.
Needing to handle everything perfectly and naturally. If there were any problems, the families would file complaints immediately, and in the worst cases even leading to lawsuits.
Those families would shove the duties and care they themselves should have provided on her, and would take advantage of their position as paying customers to put unreasonable pressure on her.
Plus, it continued getting worse year after year... slowly tearing her pride and sense of purpose as a nurse to pieces.
On top of that, the number of active nurses had decreased in recent years due to cost cutting measures. Instead of getting a weekly day off, she'd work overtime every single week.
And furthermore, even though she worked so hard and contributed so much... medical association policy limited salary reviews.
Since it arrived at a time when she was already at the limit of her patience, there was no way she could resist biting at this delicious proposition.
After this, she was planning to lay low in a foreign country until things cooled down. And then it would be nice to get re-employed at another hospital...

``...But I do feel sorry for the dead boy's girlfriend.@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3010. Even though she came from so far to see him, I made her the first to discover the body.@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3011. If only it had happened on another day...``

``There was no other way.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3009. The patient was getting back to a normal way of thinking, and he heard all those stories from the girl in that other incident.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3010. This was a necessary measure to prevent information from leaking.``
``Of course, if the fire drill scheduled for the day before hadn't been postponed, it may not have been necessary to resort to such roundabout means.``
``...Indeed.``
The malfunction of the fire alarm had been a last resort she'd thought up on the spot.
She was worried the plan wouldn't work out without collaborators familiar with the mechanism, but those agents had been pros.
...It was true that this man smiling in front of her was full of mysteries, and beyond fishy.
But even so, she was promised big money and plane tickets to get out of here, so she figured she could trust him enough.
``For now... what do you say to a celebratory toast? Though it may be in poor taste to call it that.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3013. Unfortunately, I only have paper cups, but this is well-aged wine.``
``Oh my... is that Bordeaux?@k@vS20/00/VTT_hiranuma3014. It's my first time actually seeing it... Hehe, it smells lovely.``
``There are mountains of wine like this where you're going.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3015. But first, let's have a drink.``
``Yes... thank you for the wine.``
Then, they each held a paper cup filled to the brim in hand and lightly clapped them together.
Unfortunately, it lacked the clinking sound of glass, but she thought it would be nice to savor it slowly at a restaurant in her destination.
And then, after drinking the contents, her contact stood up and said that he needed to make a phone call before departure.
``...@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3017.Yes, I made her drink the sample just now.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3018. Yes, I didn't leave any fingerprints behind.``
He held the receiver to his ear while slowly peeling the resin film from his fingertips.
Of course, he wasn't foolish enough to throw them in the trash, so he stuffed them into his waist pocket.

``...That's right.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3020. It seems like the First Investigation Division at prefectural HQ is on to something, so they may arrive before she boards the plane.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3021. Hehehe... Either way, I'm sure it'll be a huge commotion.``

``Being fed up with the piling stress of being overworked, then killing a patient to take her mind off it.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3023. And then, after being cornered by the police, exploding in a panic before committing suicide...@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3024. Doesn't that sound like a rather convincing scenario?``
``...Ahahaha, I'm hurt you'd say that to me.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3026. And in the end, I let her drink some fine wine.``
``I think that's a suitable parting gift on the journey to death.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3028. Well, at the very least, it's an act of pity...``
``...That's right, I've already recovered the bankbook for the fictitious account.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3030. I thought it would get more significant use, but only a few withdrawals were made.``

``...My my, how sad it is that poor people don't understand how to use money.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3032. She probably got scared about having more money than she'd ever seen before.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3033. ...Alright then.``
``Anyway, it's about time.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3035. ...Hmm?``
Just then, a large crowd of policemen ran down the walkway next to the payphone.
And beyond that flow, a loud commotion could be heard.
``...Perfect timing, right on cue.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3037. Alright, Youko Hiranuma-san, have a nice trip.@k@vS20/32/VTT_hanada3038. Hehe... Aahahahahahahaha.``
